Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to transform a raw Windows system activity log into a structured process mining event log This involves identifying cases process instances defining activities standardized actions and extracting relevant attributes timestamps case IDs etc

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Input Data Format Timestamp ActionType Attributes Actions FOCUS TYPING SAVE SWITCH CLICK SCROLL HIGHLIGHT CLOSE Applications Microsoft Word Google Chrome Adobe Acrobat Microsoft Excel Time Span 08 59 50 to 09 08 15 Context A user working on documents emails and reports

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Case Identification Strategy Option A One Case Per Session Option B One Case Per Document Task Option C One Case Per Application Switch

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Decision Option B Task Based is the most valuable for process mining I need to group events that logically belong together

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task 1 Drafting Document1 docx Task 2 Replying to Email about Annual Meeting Task 3 Reviewing Report Draft pdf Task 4 Updating Budget 2024 xlsx Task 5 Finalizing Quarterly Report docx

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait looking closer at the sequence 1 09 00 to 09 01 Working on Document1 docx 2 09 01 45 to 09 03 20 Email interaction 3 09 04 to 09 05 Reviewing PDF 4 09 05 00 to 09 05 45 Excel Budget update 5 09 06 00 to 09 06 30 Back to Document1 docx 6 09 07 00 to 09 08 15 Switch to Quarterly Report docx

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Refinement on Cases Case 1 Drafting Document 1 Includes initial typing switching away coming back and closing Case 2 Email Correspondence Open email reply send Case 3 PDF Review Scroll highlight Case 4 Budget Update Type save Case 5 Quarterly Report Type save close

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait is Drafting Document 1 one case or two It starts at 09 00 Interrupted by Email Resumed at 09 06 00 Ends at 09 07 00 Process Mining Logic Usually interruptions break a case unless the tool supports interruption handling

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction If I put the Email actions inside Case 1 the flow looks like Type Email Type Save This implies Email is part of the Document process

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Best Practice One Case Per Logical Task

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task 1 Document 1 Drafting Initial Task 2 Email Reply Task 3 PDF Review Task 4 Budget Update Task 5 Document 1 Finalization Task 6 Quarterly Report

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Actually standard process mining does not like gaps If I put the Email events inside the Document1 case it looks like the email was a step in the document creation That is false

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case Document File Combination Case Doc1 Work All events involving Document1 docx Case Email Work All events involving Email Inbox Case PDF Work All events involving Report Draft pdf Case Budget Work All events involving Budget 2024 xlsx Case Quarterly Work All events involving Quarterly Report docx

Name: Validation - PE
Text: If I group all Document1 events into Case Doc1 the process miner sees Save Switch Switch Focus Type Close This shows the interruption

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait one more check The instruction says a case can represent a logical unit of user work Drafting Document 1 is interrupted by Replying to Email These are two different processes

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So Document1 Part 1 is Case A Email is Case B Document1 Part 2 is Case C

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait if I make them separate cases I lose the link

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Best Compromise I will create Task Based Cases Task Doc1 Drafting Task Email Reply Task PDF Review Task Budget Update Task Doc1 Follow Up Task Quarterly Report

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Standard tools prefer one Case ID per logical instance I will create 6 Cases Case Doc1 Initial Case Email Reply Case PDF Review Case Budget Update Case Doc1 Finalize Case Quarterly Report

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Activity Naming FOCUS Open Document TYPING Edit Content SAVE Save Document SWITCH Switch Application CLICK Open Email or Send Email SCROLL Review Document HIGHLIGHT Mark Important CLOSE Close Document

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Doc1 Initial 09 00 00 to 09 01 15 Ends at Save Case 2 Email 09 01 45 to 09 03 20 Starts at Switch Case 3 PDF 09 04 00 to 09 05 00 Starts at Switch Case 4 Budget 09 05 00 to 09 05 45 Starts at Focus Case 5 Doc1 Final 09 06 00 to 09 07 00 Starts at Switch Case 6 Quarterly 09 07 15 to 09 08 15 Starts at Focus

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I will assign SWITCH to the Destination case because it represents the initiation of the new context

Name: Validation - PE
Text: There is no SWITCH immediately before 09 05 00 FOCUS Excel so for Case 4 Budget the start event is FOCUS

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Finalizing Activity Names FOCUS to Focus Window TYPING to Edit Content SAVE to Save Work SWITCH to Switch Application CLICK to Perform Action SCROLL to Navigate Document HIGHLIGHT to Mark Information CLOSE to Close Window

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Constructing the Table Columns Case ID Activity Timestamp Application Window Rows Iterate through the log assigning Case IDs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1 Case Doc1 Draft 1 09 00 00 Focus Word 09 00 30 Edit Content 09 01 00 Edit Content 09 01 15 Save Work Case 2 Case Email Reply 09 01 45 Switch Application 09 02 00 Perform Action Open Email 09 02 30 Navigate Document 09 02 45 Perform Action Reply 09 03 00 Edit Content 09 03 20 Perform Action Send Case 3 Case PDF Review 09 04 00 Switch Application 09 04 30 Navigate Document 09 04 45 Mark Information Case 4 Case Budget Update 09 05 00 Focus Window 09 05 15 Edit Content 09 05 30 Edit Content 09 05 45 Save Work Case 5 Case Doc1 Draft 2 09 06 00 Switch Application 09 06 15 Edit Content 09 06 30 Save Work 09 07 00 Close Window Case 6 Case Quarterly Report 09 07 15 Focus Window 09 07 45 Edit Content 09 08 00 Save Work 09 08 15 Close Window

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Review against Constraints Case ID unique Yes Activity Name descriptive Yes Timestamp included Yes Explanation provided Yes Narrative coherent Yes

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Merging Document1 into one case makes more sense for lifecycle but this is bad because it implies Email is a step in Document creation Therefore splitting them is the correct approach for accurate process discovery

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Best for Analysis Splitting is safer to avoid noise in the process map
